tool.name: splitter
tool.summary: Partition an input sequence stream into deterministic fixed-size chunks

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `splitter` partitions an input record stream into fixed-size groups while preserving input order. The EMBOSS-RS v1 implementation models this as deterministic sequence partitions rather than as side-effectful file fan-out, which keeps the method reusable through the shared service and bridge layers.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts one local sequence input and one positive `chunk-size`. Multi-record sequence inputs are supported through the shared sequence IO path. A chunk size of `1` produces one record per partition, while the final partition may be smaller than the requested chunk size.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The service payload is a deterministic partitioned sequence result in which outer partition ordering and inner record ordering both follow the original input stream. CLI rendering remains sequence-oriented rather than creating multiple output files in v1.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs splitter`. Validation currently covers deterministic partition sizes against a committed three-record FASTA fixture, and Rust service tests exercise the same repository-managed case.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release does not create numbered output files or support multiple simultaneous chunking policies. It reports logical partitions only, and it rejects chunk sizes smaller than `1`.

artifacts.label: Three-record FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed FASTA fixture used for deterministic splitter validation.

examples.title: Partition a three-record FASTA stream into chunks of two records
examples.description: Runs `splitter` against the committed three-record FASTA fixture with chunk size `2`, producing one two-record partition and one final single-record partition.

expected_outputs.label: Deterministic sequence partitions
expected_outputs.description: A stable partition result containing records `alpha` and `beta` in the first chunk and `gamma` in the second chunk.
